review:
The hubby and I had a great time at R2L for dinner. He booked ahead for a south facing window table on 3/3/19 so we could get a gorgeous sun set dinner view. Unfortunately, that day it sleeted, cloud coverage and fog. I still enjoyed the food, the service was top notch, and the atmosphere was very romantic. Everyone from downstairs to hostess to server gave us a smile and was very polite and helpful. 

For our meal we were brought out 2 small bites from the kitchen which I believe was some type of salami with mustard topped with garlic confit and sliced almonds & micro greens over toasted slice of baguette; was delish! We also we brought out the standard bread, which were two cute little baguettes; funny part was it was on an IKEA cutting board. For drinks we both had water, and I got the "live long and..." and he got the Elder Fashion. They were both really good, his was nice n strong. We ordered the hot seafood boil appetizer, which was a nice portion, I could've just had that alone as my entrée. We loved the shishito peppers that came with it and have been obsessed ever since; and I highly recommend getting this and letting your mini baguette sop up all the liquid. Hubby got the 28day dry aged rib eye, I opted for the braised rib & filet duo, and we shared a side of crispy Brussels sprouts. I thought this wasn't going to be enough but we were at maximum capacity and had to take a small to go box home. Couldn't leave anything this good, or $$$ behind.  We did not have room for dessert but were offered by our server. 

During dinner, towards the end the sky did clear up and we were able to enjoy a beautiful night view of the city.  We had a lovely memorable dinner and will definitely return for another special occasion. 

Food:  5/5 everything came out to temp good portion & seasoned well
Atmosphere:  5/5 def a romantic date place/celebration dinner
Price: 4/5 a bit pricey if you ask me but you are paying for ambiance and service 
Service: 5/5 everyone
